title: rubus fruticosus subsp. rubeolus
intro: Rubus fruticosus subsp. rubeolus, also known as the wild raspberry, is a subspecies of the black raspberry plant that is native to North America. The medicinal properties of Rubus fruticosus subsp. rubeolus include its high content of antioxidants, flavonoids, and anthocyanins, which are known to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of consuming Rubus fruticosus subsp. rubeolus include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting digestive health, as well as its potential to prevent certain types of cancer. Rubus fruticosus subsp. rubeolus can be prepared as a tea, juice, or supplement, and is also used in herbal infusions, tinctures, and salves to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
